rulings.Case Title: SUSAN HETZEL VS. KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Apportionment Of Settlement Under Code Of Civil Procedure Section 877, Et Seq
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 21, 2017 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Hennessy Industries, LLC, erroneously sued as Hennessy Industries, Inc.'s Motion For Apportionment Of Settlement Under Code Of Civil Procedure Section 877, Et Seq. Defendant Hennessy Industries, LLC's motion for apportionment of settlement under Code of Civil Procedure section 877 is denied without prejudice to being raised before the trial judge after there has been a verdict or judgment. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: JONY WOOD ET AL VS. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 21, 2017 in Department 503, Line 2. Plaintiffs Jony Wood, et al's, Motion For Order Granting Leave To File First Amended Complaint. Plaintiffs' motion for order granting leave to file first amended complaint is granted. The First Amended Complaint is deemed served as of the date of granting this motion on all defendants who have previously appeared. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/CML)
